name: La Peñota and Peña del Águila Loop from Cercedilla
name_original: Peñota y Peña el Águila — circular desde Cercedilla
description: Hike the difficult 10.9-mile La Peñota and Peña del Águila Loop from Cercedilla, gaining 3239 feet with panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Cercedilla offers access to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, characterized by extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks like Siete Picos. The region features the Fuenfría Valley with its diverse wildlife and a network of mountain streams. Historic Roman roads and bridges are integrated into the natural landscape, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cercedilla The most popular hiking route is Ducha de Los Alemanes, Mirador de los Poetas, Reloj de Cela — circular desde Las Dehesas de Cercedilla, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, featuring viewpoints and the Las Dehesas recreational area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadarrama National Park - Route of the Seven Peaks from Puerto de Navacerrada, a difficult 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path offering views of the Siete Picos. Local hikers also love the Peñota y Peña el Águila — circular desde Cercedilla, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with peaks like Peñota and Peña del Águila, often completed in about 6 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Cercedilla is defined by dense pine forests, historic Roman roads, and mountain peaks, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cercedilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 160 trails recorded. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla is suitable for hiking year-round. The dense pine forests provide ample shade for summer hikes. Autumn transforms the forests into a haven for mushroom enthusiasts, while winter offers opportunities for snow hiking and skiing. Spring brings lush greenery and pleasant temperatures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, Cercedilla has trails suitable for families and beginners. There are 12 easy routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232281875/ducha-de-los-alemanes-mirador-de-los-poetas-reloj-de-cela-circular-desde-las-dehesas-de-cercedilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducha de Los Alemanes, Mirador de los Poetas, Reloj de Cela — circular desde Las Dehesas de Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, is a moderate option that features viewpoints and the Las Dehesas recreational area, which has amenities.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking in Cercedilla offers diverse scenery. You'll encounter extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks like Siete Picos. The Fuenfría Valley features mountain streams and small waterfalls. Historic Roman roads and bridges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, are also integrated into the landscape. Many routes offer stunning views from various viewpoints like Mirador de la Pelona and Poets' Viewpoint.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, many trails in Cercedilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230613754/penota-y-pena-el-aguila-circular-desde-cercedilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñota y Peña el Águila — circular desde Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain with peaks like Peñota and Peña del Águila.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trailheads in Cercedilla using public transport?
A: Cercedilla is well-connected and accessible from Madrid, located just 56 kilometers away. The town is served by a special 'Nature Train' (Tren de la Naturaleza) that runs from Cercedilla to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de los Cotos&lt;/a&gt;, offering guided tours and access to various trailheads within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Where can I find parking near the hiking trails?
A: The Las Dehesas Recreational Area, located near Cercedilla, is a popular starting point for many trails and provides amenities, which typically include parking facilities. This area is a convenient base for exploring the surrounding nature.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Cercedilla?
A: The trails in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dense pine forests, the historical Roman roads, and the breathtaking mountain peaks, highlighting the diverse and engaging terrain for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Cercedilla offers 45 difficult routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956998731/guadarrama-national-park-route-of-the-seven-peaks-from-puerto-de-navacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama National Park - Route of the Seven Peaks from Puerto de Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.6-mile path offering spectacular views of the Siete Picos. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956994549/guadarrama-national-park-from-cercedilla-to-navacerrada-pass-via-schmid-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama National Park - from Cercedilla to Navacerrada Pass via Schmid path&lt;/a&gt;, a 26 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Cercedilla after a hike?
A: Cercedilla is a mountain town with various amenities. The Las Dehesas Recreational Area offers kiosks, and the town itself provides cafes, pubs, and accommodation options for hikers looking to relax and refuel after their adventures.

Q: Are there any natural pools or swimming spots near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, in the summer, Cercedilla's natural pools, fed by fresh mountain waters, become a popular attraction. These offer a refreshing dip and are a great way to cool down after a hike, particularly in warmer months.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The Fuenfría Valley, a significant environmental unit within the Sierra de Guadarrama, is home to diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot roe deer, wild boar, badgers, foxes, squirrels, and various species of birds, amphibians, and reptiles within the extensive Scots pine forests.
tips: It's cool to stop and listen to the water fall

La Peñota, with its 1945 meters of altitude, rises between the Castilian and Madrid plateaus. The peak is a spectacular pile of stacked granite masses. La Peñota marks the limits of the Sierra de Guadarrama national park.
La Peña del Águila is located 2008 meters above sea level and enjoys impressive views of the entire Sierra de Guadarrama national park. Especially the Peñalara peak, the highest in Madrid.

The Marichiva pass is a traditional pass between the Fuenfría and Espinar valleys, it is marked with the red and white chevrons of the GR-10 long-distance trail on the popular Calle Alta forest track, along the route of the old Vereda del Infante, very popular with fans of mountain biking.

faqs.question: How challenging is the La Peñota and Peña del Águila Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is classified as difficult and requires a good physical condition. It involves significant elevation gain and varied terrain, including steep ascents and potentially rugged paths. Hikers should be prepared for a demanding experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and often demanding. You'll hike through dense pine forests and oak groves in lower sections, transitioning to more exposed areas with granite rock formations as you ascend. Expect steep sections, including parts known as the 'ladera del infierno', and some rugged, vertical paths. The crest between the peaks features a stone wall.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers excellent panoramic views. You'll enjoy vistas of the Fuenfría Valley, Hoya Moros Valley, and distant peaks like Mujer Muerta, La Maliciosa, and Peñalara. Key landmarks include the La Peñota Peak and Eagle Rock, the Marichiva Pass, and the iconic 'Pino Solitario' (Solitary Pine). You might also spot the Deer Petroglyph on Los Poyalejos Trail.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically from late spring to early autumn when the weather is milder and the trails are clear of snow and ice. During winter, the trail can be very challenging due to snow, ice, and strong winds, requiring specialized equipment like snowshoes or crampons.

faqs.question: Are there any historical features along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of ancient historical paths. You'll find remnants of the Roman road that once connected Titulcia with Segovia, and parts of the 18th-century Bourbon road, built for access to the Royal Palace of La Granja de San Ildefonso. The Deer Petroglyph on Los Poyalejos Trail is also a notable historical site.

faqs.question: Is the La Peñota and Peña del Águila Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Guadarrama, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Given the trail's difficult rating and rugged terrain, ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, challenging hike and bring plenty of water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail in Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and packing out all trash.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Cercedilla?
faqs.answer: Cercedilla offers several parking options, particularly near the train station or the Fuenfría Valley entrance. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as parking can fill up quickly for popular trails like this one. Public transport via train to Cercedilla is also a good alternative.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It is rated as 'difficult' with significant elevation gain and challenging terrain. Hikers should have a good level of fitness and prior hiking experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route crosses and follows sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the GR-10 trail, the 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde el Puerto de la Fuenfría a San Lorenzo del Escorial)', 'Camino de Campamentos', 'Valle de la Fuenfría', and the 'Camino Puricelli'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, essential items include sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water (at least 2-3 liters), snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), layers of clothing for changing weather, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. In winter, add crampons, an ice axe, and warm, waterproof clothing.

waypoints.name: Deer Petroglyph on Los Poyalejos Trail, Cercedilla
